title: Botrychium microphyllum
taxonomy: Botrychium microphyllum belongs to the family Ophioglossaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as adder's tongue ferns. This species is a type of terrestrial fern, also known as a "fiddlehead" fern. Within the family Ophioglossaceae, Botrychium microphyllum is classified as a genus of small to medium-sized ferns. It is native to the Americas and is typically found in moist, shaded environments. The classification of Botrychium microphyllum into the broader categories of kingdom and phylum places it within Plantae, and further into Tracheophyta and Polypodiophyta.
uses: Botrychium microphyllum, also known as dwarf rattlesnake plantain, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Traditionally, it has been used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as wounds and burns. The plant's medicinal properties are thought to be due to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and terpenoids. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Botrychium microphyllum in modern medicine.

parts: Botrychium microphyllum, also known as the least grape fern, is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine. intro: Botrychium microphyllum, also known as small-leaved grape fern or grape fern, is a type of rare, perennial, and deciduous fern native to North America. The health benefits of Botrychium microphyllum include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a promising natural remedy for cardiovascular health.
